mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and equity deleveraging that depresses S&P 500 prices within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the employment drop, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit availability and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, leading to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both series decline simultaneously, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative during consecutive months of rising INDPRO, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth below the historical trend rate signals weakening aggregate demand, which compresses earnings growth forecasts and forces equity multiples lower over a quarterly period.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth misses the 1.5% threshold, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance in a bootstrap test.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate acts as a lagging indicator of economic stress, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and causing downward pressure on S&P 500 valuations within a single week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows no negative correlation between UNRATE increases and 5-day SPX returns, or the signal produces positive returns in over 50% of rising-unemployment months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of headline inflation and federal funds rate creates a real rate shock that elevates discount rates faster than earnings can grow, mechanically reducing equity present values and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing permits forecasts a structural slowdown in residential investment and related consumer spending, which reduces corporate cash flow projections and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where HOUST drops below 850, or the signal shows no predictive power in a rolling 5-year out-of-sample test.
